title: Getting a haircut at a local salon
description: Tell the stylist what cut you want and chat while they work
context: Neighbourhood hair salons (髮廊) in Hong Kong are a social hub as much as a service. The stylist will chat throughout your cut — it's considered friendly. Be clear about what you want: length, style, fringe or no fringe.
cultural_note: Alongside modern salons, Hong Kong still has traditional 理髮廳 (lei5 faat3 teng1), old-style barbershops marked by the rotating red-white-blue pole. Some of these barbers have worked the same chair for forty years. The standard service includes a close shave, ear cleaning, and a scalp massage that feels like a medical procedure. Most cost under HK$80.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, ngo5 soeng2 zin2 tau4 faat3.
lines.english: Excuse me, I'd like a haircut.

lines.speaker: stylist
lines.jyutping: hou2, co5 dai1 sin1. soeng2 dim2 zin2?
lines.english: Sure, have a seat. How would you like it cut?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 剪短啲，但係唔好太短。兩邊打薄啲。
lines.jyutping: zin2 dyun2 di1, daan6 hai6 m4 hou2 taai3 dyun2. loeng5 bin1 daa2 bok6 di1.
lines.english: Shorter, but not too short. Thin out the sides.

lines.speaker: stylist
lines.jyutping: hou2, lau4 m4 lau4 lau4 hoi2?
lines.english: OK, do you want a fringe?

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: m4 sai2 lau4 lau4 hoi2, m4 goi1.

lines.speaker: stylist
lines.jyutping: zin2 hou2 laa3, tai2 haa5 dim2 aa3?
lines.english: All done. Have a look, what do you think?

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: hou2 leng3 aa3! gei2 do1 cin2?
